urdu transliteration text:
Going Into The Bazaar Of Shaam Without A Covering, Ask Sajjad
Be Parda Haram Shaam Ke Bazaaro Me Jana, Sajjad Se Pucho
Aur Naam E Nabi Zadi Ka Darbar Me Aana, Sajjad Se Pucho
english translation text: And Hearing The Name Of The Granddaughter Of The Prophet In The Court, Ask Sajjad

urdu transliteration text: Chalees Shabo Roz Ki Jaagi Hui Zainab Gash Khake Giri Jab. Us Waqt Sitamgaar Ka Durro Se Jagana, Sajjad Se Pucho
english translation text: The grief to see Zainab fainting and falling down after being awake for 40 days and nights; And at that moment to be awoken by the whip of the  tyrant; Ask Sajjad

urdu transliteration text: Masoom Sakina Ne Jo Haatho Ko Uthaaya Baba Ko Bulaya. Beti Ki Sada Par Sare Shabbir Ka Aana, Sajjad Se Pucho
english translation text: The Innocent Sakina Raised Her Hands And Asked For Her Father To Come Back, By The Call Of Sakina The Head Of Hussain Came Instead, Ask Sajjad

urdu transliteration text: Maa Behno Ke Sar Ko Jo Khula Dekh Na Paya Haider Ka Dulaara. Naize Se Zami Par Sare Abbas Ka Aana, Sajjad Se Pucho
english translation text: The Son Of Haider Could Not Bear Seeing The Uncovered Heads Of The Mothers And Sister, At That Moment, Abbas' Head Fell Onto The Ground, Ask Sajjad

urdu transliteration text: Tareek Ye Zindan Woh Sakina Woh Andhera Jab Maut Ne Ghera. Un Akhri Lamhaat Me Baba Ko Bulanaa, Sajjad Se Pucho
english translation text: The darkness of the dungeon where young Sakina passed away, calling out to her beloved father Hussain (a.s) in her last moments, Ask Sajjad

urdu transliteration text: Rota Tha Kabhi Soch Ke Woh Alaame Gurbat Har Lamha Qayamat. Ankho Se Lahu Kalb Ka Har Gham Bahana. Sajjad Se Pucho
english translation text: He cried every moment, thinking of the sufferings (of the family of Prophet), how painful it is to weep the tears of blood; Ask Sajjad
